text:
There are numerous real-life weapons featured in the Hunter × Hunter world.

Melee Weapons

Kurapika uses a pair of bokken multiple times before Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 2 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 3 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 4 and during Hunter × Hunter - Volume 2, Chapter 9 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 4, Chapter 27 the 287th Hunter Exam. Inside Trick Tower, Togari uses four curved knives to fight Hisoka. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 3, Chapter 19 They may be based on the karambit or jambiya.

When he and Kurapika are confronted by Hisoka during the Fourth Phase of the exam, Leorio draws a switchblade to defend himself. During the final phase of the exam, Hanzo reveals a long blade hidden under the bandage on his right forearm, which he uses to threaten Gon. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 4, Chapter 34 

As Seaquant recounts bringing 100 thugs to the Zoldyck Family estate and being defeated by Canary, a flashback shows the wounded men lying on the ground with various swords, spears, and shields among them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 41 During Gotoh's coin game, two of the Zoldyck butlers pull out large knives, one of whom holds the blade at Canary's throat. Later, Gon asks to borrow Leorio's switchblade and uses it to cut his swollen eye in order to reduce the swelling. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 43 

Inside the mansion of the Nostrade Family, eight of Tocino's puppets that attack the others are armed with swords. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 67 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 68 Nobunaga carries a katana on his left hip in his obi sash. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 92 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 112 When fighting alongside Uvogin (who was wielding two larges clubs) in the past Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 93 and at the time of his arrival in Yorknew City, Nobunaga also carried a second katana, identical to the first. Bonolenov wears a pair of boxing gloves, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 71 but they are only used to cover the holes in his hands, not as weapons. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 225 

When the Mafia prepares to confront several members of the Phantom Troupe in the Gordeau Desert, one Mafia member is seen wielding a long sword. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 75 After their group captures Uvogin, Dalzollene tries to stab the Troupe member with a katana, but he activates his Nen and causes the blade to break. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 78 As Zenji punches Light Nostrade multiple times, Kurapika draws a switchblade and holds it to his throat. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 95 

Assassin A tracks down Chrollo inside the Cemetery Building and readies a combat knife after the Troupe leader proposes a one-on-one fight. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 96 

Saccho carries two katana on his left hip in his obi sash. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 30, Chapter 318 In Swardani City, the Hunters sent after Illumi are seen carrying various weapons such as clubs and katana. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 328 

Aboard the Black Whale, Sayird is forced to use a knife and kill three of Prince Woble's guards (including Kurton), Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 with Kurapika quickly disarming him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 361 After Vincent enters Woble's quarters, he says that Sandra came at him with a knife and he only defended himself, with the weapon seen inside the maid's chest. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 On Tier 3 of the ship, Luini uses a knife to kill twenty people before making his way to Tier 5 and killing at least three Cha-R Family members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 379 

In Prince Tserriednich's quarters on Tier 1, several bloodied and damaged weapons are seen lying on the floor as his guards mop up what appear to be human remains. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 384 On Tier 3, Bille of the Heil-Ly Family uses a knife to cut Zakuro's neck. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 390 

Projectile Weapons

During the Fourth Phase of the exam, Pokkle uses a bow with drugged arrows to immobilize Kyu and take his badge. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 3, Chapter 24 Geretta's primary weapon is a blowgun that he loads with paralyzing darts, using it on Gon to retrieve his and Hisoka's badges. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 4, Chapter 28 

Three of Tocino's puppets use handguns to shoot at the other bodyguards, killing the Nostrade butler in the process. Kurapika is able to use his chain to stop multiple bullets. When the Mafia confronts several Troupe members in the Gordeau Desert, many are armed with handguns. One shoots Uvogin at point-blank range, but he catches the bullet with his teeth. After Uvogin kills two snipers with rocks, a Mafia member appears with a bazooka and boasts that it can destroy a tank. Although Uvogin says that it hurt, he easily survives the blast. 

Several police officers are seen around the Cemetery Building. One Mafia member is seen holding an Uzi as he talks with a policeman holding a shotgun. Inside the building, several Mafia members are armed with various assault rifles. During the Troupe's requiem, the Mafia members use a variety of firearms (including submachine guns to attack the Spiders, but to no avail. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 97 While being controlled by Shalnark, one Mafia member uses what resembles an MP 40 to kill at least one of his comrades before being killed by them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 98 

Several Mafia members are armed with weapons when they find the Troupe members' fake corpses. Zenji uses a handgun and threatens to shoot Kurapika in the head, but Kurapika's bloodlust leaves the Mafia member frozen with fear. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 101 Pakunoda's Nen ability involves the use of a revolver to implant memories in others with conjured bullets. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 105 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 119 

Inside the nest of the Chimera Ants, Peggy shows Colt at least one soldier Ant who was killed by a gun. Elsewhere in NGL, an Ant is shown wielding two handguns after using them on humans. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 19, Chapter 189 Soon afterwards, the Ant uses one of the guns to kill Ponzu. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 19, Chapter 190 Before discovering the D² Manufacturing Plant, Killua finds two assault rifles in the nearby forest. Inside the facility, one assault rifle is seen lying on the ground. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 19, Chapter 192 At the royal palace of East Gorteau, Meruem encounters several soldiers armed with assault rifles and easily kills them all. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 216 

In Pata City, multiple police officers fire their handguns at Cheetu, but the Ant easily dodges the bullets and bites off their fingers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 218 In Peijin, a soldier manipulated by Neferpitou uses a machine gun to shoot at Morel, who dodges the bullets and strikes him with his pipe. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 23, Chapter 244 Later, two other soldiers fire at Knov, who uses his Hide and Seek ability to escape. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 23, Chapter 246 

As the citizens of East Gorteau begin the forced march toward the palace, several soldiers armed with assault rifles are seen watching them from the rooftops. An announcer declares that anyone who steps out of line will be shot on sight. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 259 The soldiers continue to order the citizens as they approach the palace. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 260 During his encounter with Ikalgo, Welfin remembers the Ants attacking the D² Manufacturing Plant, with Gyro's men shown using handguns and machine guns to defend themselves. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 28, Chapter 296 In the montage showing the cruel nature of humanity, multiple assault rifles, submachine guns, and handguns can be seen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 30, Chapter 311 

In Swardani City, one of the Hunters sent after Illumi is seen carrying a bow and arrows. 

Firearms are featured heavily in the Succession Contest arc:

Aboard the Black Whale, several soldiers from Kakin's Royal Army are shown holding assault rifles and guarding the throughways between the tiers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

After Kurapika discovers four more dead guards in Woble's quarters, he pulls out a handgun and points it at the remaining four guards, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 questioning them while using his Dowsing Chain. After Sayrid kills three of the guards with a knife, Bill pulls out a handgun and threatens to shoot him if he doesn't stop. However, Kurapika says that he wants Sayrid alive and tells Bill that he can't stop a Hunter with a .22 caliber pistol. When soldiers arrive to remove the bodies, several of them are armed with assault rifles. 

In the VVIP living quarters, several soldiers armed with assault rifles are seen stationed in the outer hallway. 

After Vincent enters Woble's quarters and pulls out a handgun, Kurapika distracts him as Bill charges toward him and blocks multiple bullets with his Nen. With Kurapika drawing his own weapon, he and Bill take subdue Vincent and force the gun out of his hand. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 364 

During Kurapika's Nen class, Sakata uses a handgun to shoot the snakes that killed Barrigen. Kurapika notes that Prince Zhang Lei's guards also use 9mm Luger, telling himself that Gyo won't be enough to remain unscathed. He also worries about encountering .45 caliber or Heckler & Koch. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 

With his handgun and Dowsing Chain drawn, Kurapika asks Shimano about the attack on Barrigen and whether she can use Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 

When Prince Camilla confronts Musse, he draws his handgun and shoots her twice in the chest and once in the head after she continues walking toward him. After killing Musse and reviving herself with her ability, Camilla picks up his weapon and tells her armed guards that she will take care of the situation. She then encounters Furykov and Wolfe, using the gun to kill the latter and barely wound the former after he used Nen. After she reaches Benjamin's quarters, Camilla fires off multiple rounds at her brother, but they are all blocked. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 

In the central dining hall on Tier 5, several Mafia members with handguns drawn surround the members of the Phantom Troupe. Their leader makes sure that his men don't use their weapons when Chrollo initially refuses his order. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 

After Luini's attack on Tier 3, several armed soldiers are seen at the crime scene. On Tier 5, Ken'i Wang and his subordinates (all of whom are armed with submachine guns) confront Phinks, Nobunaga, and Feitan. 

While looking for a stowaway with a deadly weapon, Botobai leads several armed soldiers as they begin their sweep on Tier 3, looking through beds and conducting interviews. On the observation deck, soldiers with their rifles drawn find Illumi and Kalluto, who quickly put their hands up. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 

Outside his father's chambers, Halkenburg and five of his guards pull out handguns and point them at the desk clerk and the three soldiers with their assault rifles hanging by their hips. Halkenburg mentions that it took four days to get them to lower their weapons. Inside, Nasubi refuses to suspend the contest for succession, and Halkenburg fires one shot, with the bullet stopping inches from the King's head. Nasubi tells his son that he should always fire three shots when shooting to kill. Halkenburg then puts the gun against his own head and tries to kill himself to end the contest, but his Guardian Spirit Beast stops the bullet. Halkenburg drops the gun and leaves his father's chambers now resolved to become King so that he can change Kakin. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 382 

While Melody plays her flute solo during the Sunday banquet, several soldiers are seen lying unconscious on the floor with their assault rifles next to them. After Princes Kacho and Fugetsu escape in a lifeboat, Keeney pulls out a handgun and shoots himself in the head since any accomplices would affect the entire mission for the Hunter Association. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 383 

During Prince Tserriednich's Zetsu training, Theta pulls out a handgun and appears to kill him with a single headshot. However, it's revealed that Tserriednich is still alive as two of his guards enter the room with their own guns drawn. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 385 The same events are then seen from Tserriednich's perspective. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 387 

Outside of Prince Luzurus' quarters, Sumidori (inhabiting Shikaku's body) pulls out a handgun and shoots himself in the head. Luzurus' guards draw their weapons after hearing the shot in the hallway. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 386 

As Zhang Lei leaves his quarters to visit Onior, he passes by at least 17 soldiers armed with assault rifles. Accompanied by Zakuro and Lynch, Hinrigh pays two armed soldiers so that the three Mafia members can make their way up to Tier 3. When Hinrigh tries to follow a member of the Heil-Ly Family, two soldiers block his path, one armed with an assault rifle and the other with a submachine gun. Later, two soldiers tell them to leave Tier 3, and Hinrigh touches their guns while giving them a bribe. Soon afterwards, their weapons turn into snakes and kill both of them. It's revealed that Hinrigh's ability allows him to transform a weapon or piece of machinery into an animal by touching it, with the creature retaining the weapon or machinery's original functionality. 

Military Vehicles

Armored Vehicles

In Peijin, multiple tanks are shown as the manipulated corpse of Ming Jol-ik's Double places East Gorteau under martial law. Killua is confident that he can take them all down. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 234 As the citizens of East Gorteau begin the forced march toward the palace, tanks are seen accompanying them. Underneath the royal palace of East Gorteau, Ikalgo uses an armored transportation vehicle to fight against Bloster. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 282 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 283 

Military Aircraft

When East Gorteau is placed under martial law, Killua is confident that he can take down all their fighter aircraft. 

References
